## [LISC, Cook County Bureau of Economic Development, and Cook County Arts Announce Recipients of Creative Placemaking Grants](https://www.lisc.org/chicago/regional-stories/lisc-cook-county-bureau-economic-development-and-cook-county-arts-announce-recipients-creative-placemaking-grants/)

LISC, in collaboration with Cook County Bureau of Economic Development and Cook County Arts, today announced 14 nonprofit organizations selected to receive Creative Placemaking grants. The grants, ranging from $50,000 to $250,000, will support transformative, community-driven art projects across suburban Cook County, including public murals, community gardens, arts centers, and more.

**6.17.2025**

**[LISC Stories](https://www.lisc.org/chicago/regional-stories/?globalCategory=lisc-stories),** **[Economic Development](https://www.lisc.org/chicago/regional-stories/?localCategory=economic-development-chicago)**

## [Celebrating 20 Years of Financial Opportunity in Chicago](https://www.lisc.org/chicago/regional-stories/celebrating-20-years-financial-opportunity-chicago/)

This year, we’re proud to celebrate 20 years of implementing the Financial Opportunity Center® (FOC) model—a model that does just that. Born out of the vision of community leaders and residents across our neighborhoods, the FOC model is rooted in coaching—not counseling.

[VIEW MORE](https://www.lisc.org/chicago/regional-stories/)
